purpose: COS.Lending.Selling.WebApi is a .NET-based service that manages the selling and purchase of loans between marketplace lenders (MPLs) and investors. It handles loan lifecycle management including funding, purchasing, interest accrual, and fee collections, serving as a core component in Cross River Bank's lending platform.
businessFeatures: Fee calculation and collection (Volume fees, DMV fees, Florida stamp tax, etc.)

apis.purpose: Get details for a specific loan
Generate pre-sale or post-sale loan reports

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Represents a loan with its sale status, type and associated details

dataEntities.name: LoanAccount
dataEntities.description: Account information associated with a loan for different purposes

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Agreement between MPL and issuing bank containing fee calculation methods

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Money transfer between accounts with status tracking

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Records actions performed on loans like daily interest accrual

dataEntities.name: Batch
dataEntities.description: Group of loans being processed together

dataEntities.name: VolumeFeeCalculationMethod
dataEntities.description: Defines how fees are calculated for loans

messagingPatterns.name: DailyInterestOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Ensures reliable processing of daily interest accruals

messagingPatterns.name: TransferOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Handles asynchronous money transfers between accounts

messagingPatterns.name: FeeOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Manages fee collections (DMV, Florida Stamp Tax, etc.)

messagingPatterns.name: NotificationOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Sends notifications about events via hooks service

messagingPatterns.name: ReportingOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Handles generation of pre-sale and post-sale reports

findings.title: Multi-tenancy enforcement issues
findings.description: The PolicyTenantService doesn't validate if the tenant has proper access to resources in some paths. Some tenant validation might be bypassed, particularly in the CRB tenant identification logic which relies only on string matching of policy names rather than proper role-based checks.

findings.title: Manual transaction management
findings.description: Various processors manage database transactions manually, which could lead to inconsistent state if errors occur during processing. Consider implementing a more robust transaction management approach with proper rollback mechanisms.

findings.title: Hardcoded credentials in docker-compose
findings.description: The docker-compose.yml file contains hardcoded database credentials. These should be externalized using environment variables or a secrets management solution.
